content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

June 20, 1996

Clyde Eller
Supervisor, District 4
County of Del Norte
583 "G" Street, Suite #1
Crescent City, California 95531

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-96-133

Dear Mr. Eller:

This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act")1 as they pertain to your position as a Del Norte County
Supervisor.

QUESTIONS

1) May you participate in any Board of Supervisor's decision
regarding the construction of Pelican Bay State Prison II?

2) If the Board of Supervisors decides to build Pelican Bay
State Prison II ("PBSP II"), may you participate in the decision
to request from the Department of Corrections and/or the State
Legislature that PBSP II be located in Del Norte County?

3) If the Board of Supervisors declines to decide to build
PBSP II, may you participate in the decision to place the issue on
the ballot for the electorate to decide in the November 1996
general election?

CONCLUSIONS

1) You may not participate in the decision regarding the
construction of Pelican Bay State Prison II since it will have a
material financial effect on your business, Eller's Fort Dick
Market.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. Commission regulations
appear at Title 2, Sections 18000-18995 of the California Code of
Regulations.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

File No. A-96-133
Page 2


2) You may not participate in the decision to request from
the Department of Corrections and/or the State Legislature that
PBSP II be located in Del Norte County since it will have a
material financial effect on your business.

3) You may not participate in the decision to place the
issue on the ballot for the electorate to decide in the November
1996 general election since it will have a material financial
effect on your business.

FACTS

You were elected as the Del Norte County Supervisor for
District 4 in June 1994 and assumed office in January 1995.
Pelican Bay State Prison (the "prison") is bordered on three sides
by District 4 and is located less than one mile from your
business, Eller's Port Dick Market (the "market"), a general
store, gas station and delicatessen.

You and your wife own the market as a sole proprietorship;
you also own the building and the land. The market has a gross
income of just over $1,000,000 annually and is the only store
within seven or eight miles of the prison. Much of the market's
business comes from prison employees traveling to and from that
facility and other employees who leave the prison to have meals
and/or shop at the market. Prison employees who live in the Fort
Dick area also frequent the market.

In 1993, the California Department of Corrections contracted
for an Environmental Impact Study on an area tentatively
identified for a new state prison which has been referred to as
Pelican Bay State Prison II. The selected for PBSP II is
immediately south of the existing prison and just over one mile
from your business. If construction of PBSP II is approved, it
would have a significant impact upon your business by hundreds of
thousands of dollars annually during construction and a minimum of
$50,000 to $100,000 annually after occupancy.

To date, you have not participated in any board decisions
regarding construction of PBSP II in Del Norte County.

ANALYSIS

The Act prohibits a public official from making, participat-
ing in, or using his official position to influence a governmental
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a
financial interest. (Section 87100.) A public official has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the
official or a member of his immediate family or on:

File No. A-96-133
Page 3


     (a)  Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1000) or more.

     (b)  Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1000) or more.

     (c)  Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty dol-
lars ($250) or more in value provided to, received
by or promised to the public official within 12
months prior to the time when the decision is made.

     (d)  Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

          (Section 87103(a)-(d).)

     As a member of the Del Norte County Board of Supervisors, you
may not participate in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect on any
business entity or real property in which you have an interest of
$1,000 or more.  (Section 87103(a) and (b).)  You own both the
business and property for Eller's Port Dick Market.

Foreseeability

     The effect of a decision is reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood that it will occur or a certainty is not
required; however, an effect that is merely a possibility is not
reasonably foreseeable.  (Downey Cares v. Downey Community
Development Com. (1987) 196 Cal. App. 3d 983; In re Thorner 1 FPPC
Ops. 198.)

     According to your facts, there would be a significant impact
economic impact on your market if PBSP II is built in Del Norte
County.  Since it is reasonably foreseeable that your business
would be affected by the decision to build PBSP II within such
close proximity to your business, it must be determined if the
effect will be material.

Materiality

     The Commission has adopted several regulations to determine
whether a decision will have a material financial effect.  These
regulations contain different standards depending on (1) whether
the decision pending before the Board of Supervisors directly or
indirectly affects your economic interests and (2) the type of
economic interest which would be affected by the decision.

File No. A-96-133
Page 4


     Due to your market's proximity to the selected site of PBSP
II, your business will be indirectly affected by the decision
regarding the construction of PBSP II.  Regulation 18702.2
provides guidelines for determining if the effect of a decision is
material as to a business entity in which an official has an
economic interest.  You informed us that subdivision (g) of the
regulation is the appropriate threshold to apply.  Regulation
18702.2(g) provides as follows:

     (1)  The decision will result in an increase or
decrease in the gross revenues for a fiscal year of
$10,000 or more; or

     (2)  The decision will result in the business
entity incurring or avoiding additional expenses or
reducing or eliminating existing expenses for a fiscal
year in the amount of $2,500 or more; or

     (3)  The decision will result in an increase or
decrease in the value of assets or liabilities of
$10,000 or more.

Question 1

     You have stated that the construction of PBSP II, which will
be located just over one mile from Eller's Fort Dick Market, will
increase the gross revenues of your business by more than $10,000
in a fiscal year and the value of the real property will increase
by more than $10,000.  Since construction of PBSP II at a site
about one mile from the market will have a material financial
effect upon your business, you may not participate in any
decisions regarding construction of PBSP II.

     You also own the property upon which the market is located.
You have stated that the construction of PBSP II would increase
the value of your real property by more than $10,000.  To analyze
whether there will be a material financial effect upon your
property interest, Regulation 18702.3(b), copy enclosed, would be
determinative.  We do not have enough facts to analyze if there
will be a material financial effect under this regulation,
however, a material effect upon any one of your economic interests
will prohibit your participation.

Question 2

     If the Board of Supervisors decides to build PBSP II (a
decision in which you may not participate), you also may not
participate in the board decision to request from the Department
of Corrections and/or the State Legislature that PBSP II be
located in Del Norte County.  The site for PBSP II has already
been identified by the California Department of Corrections.  The
area selected is just over one mile from your market/business.
Therefore, based upon the facts above, you also may not

File No. A-96-133
Page 5


participate in the locating decision which would have a material
financial effect on your market/business.

Question 3

If the Board of Supervisors declines to decide to build
PBSP II, you may not participate in the decision to place this
issue on the ballot for the electorate to decide in the November
1996 general election. Since it is reasonably foreseeable that
construction of PBSP II will have a material financial effect on
your market/business, you may not participate in any decision
regarding this matter.

Please be advised that "participating in the making of a
governmental decision" is defined very broadly in Regulation
18700(c), copy enclosed. In addition, you may not "use your
official position to influence" a governmental decision.
(Regulation 18700.1, copy enclosed.) However, this regulation
provides some narrow exceptions which allow you to participate as
any member of the general public (Regulation 18700.1(b) or to
communicate with the general public or the press (Regulation
18700.1(c).

I trust this answers your questions.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Jill Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JS:ak

Enclosures

May you participate in any Board of Supervisor’s decision
regarding the construction of Pelican Bay State Prison II?
2)
If the Board of Supervisors decides to build Pelican Bay
State Prison II
(“PBSP II”),
may you participate in the decision
to request from the Department of Corrections and/or the State
Legislature that PBSP II be located in Del Norte County?
3)
If the Board of Supervisors declines to decide to build
PBSP II,
may you participate in the decision to place the issue on
the ballot for the electorate to decide in the November 1996
general election?

CONCLUSION: 1)
You may not participate in the decision regarding the
construction of Pelican Bay State Prison II since it will have a
material financial effect on your business,
Eller’s Fort Dick
Market.

(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—96—l33

2)
